system.description: Ley-line wizard who sees magic in motion and rides the currents of mystic energy better than any other common spell caster.
system.notes: Use the class as a dedicated spell caster who thrives when the ley line itself becomes part of the scene.

occSkills.name: Language
occSkills.notes: Choose two additional languages at +15% each.

grantedAbilities.name: Sense Ley Line and Magic Energy
grantedAbilities.notes: The walker automatically senses ley lines, nexus points, rifts, active magic, and visible magic energy as described in the class text.

grantedAbilities.name: Ley Line Transmission
grantedAbilities.notes: Send brief messages along a ley line to others standing on the same line.

grantedAbilities.name: Ley Line Phasing
grantedAbilities.notes: Teleport along the same ley line after a short concentration, within the hourly and daily limits from the class description.

grantedAbilities.name: Line Drifting
grantedAbilities.notes: Float and travel directly along a ley line without spending personal P.P.E.

grantedAbilities.name: Ley Line Rejuvenation
grantedAbilities.notes: Rest on a ley line to heal faster, and once every 24 hours regain rest plus 2D6 hit points and 2D6 S.D.C. after ten minutes of concentration.

grantedAbilities.name: Ley Line Observation Ball
grantedAbilities.notes: Create a floating observation globe linked to the walker while remaining on a ley line.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Hand to Hand may be taken as an other skill. Basic costs one selection, Expert costs two, and Martial Arts or Assassin costs three.

unresolvedNotes: At least two related skills must be chosen from Science and one from Technical.
The walker may learn or buy additional spells at any time outside the normal level-up choices.
Roll starting money and black-market items from the class text.

system.description: Intuitive seer-mage whose power blends low-order spell casting with sensitive and healing psionics.
system.notes: Play the character as a quiet reader of omens and instincts who trusts inner certainty more than formal schooling.

grantedAbilities.name: Sense Supernatural Evil
grantedAbilities.notes: The mystic automatically feels the presence of supernatural evil and can judge whether it is near or far, weak or terrible.

grantedAbilities.name: Open to the Supernatural
grantedAbilities.notes: Enter a trance to become receptive to supernatural contact, hide from psychic probes, and melt into the surroundings while perfectly still.

grantedAbilities.name: Magic Bonus Progression
grantedAbilities.notes: Apply the horror-factor, save versus magic, and spell strength bonuses exactly as the class text lists by level.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Hand to Hand must be bought as an other skill. Basic costs one selection, Expert costs two, and Martial Arts or Assassin costs three.

unresolvedNotes: At level one, choose 1D6 additional languages manually from the class text.
The mystic cannot normally learn extra spells beyond the listed level-up choices, except rare scroll use as described in the class rules.
Roll starting money and black-market items from the class text.

system.description: Rift-focused summoner and dimensional magician who bends portals, bargains with otherworldly powers, and walks close to disaster.
system.notes: Use the class when the campaign wants dangerous magic, eerie allies, and dimensional travel to matter at the table.

occSkills.name: Language
occSkills.notes: Choose two additional languages at +15% each.

grantedAbilities.name: Dimensional Rift Home
grantedAbilities.notes: The shifter can always open the way home for themself and their familiar by paying the P.P.E. cost listed in the class text.

grantedAbilities.name: Sense Rifts
grantedAbilities.notes: Automatically feel active rifts within range and tell the general direction and size of the disturbance.

grantedAbilities.name: Familiar Link
grantedAbilities.notes: Bind a normal animal familiar and share its senses, endurance bonus, and dangers exactly as the class rules describe.

grantedAbilities.name: Link to the Supernatural
grantedAbilities.notes: A shifter may choose to bind with a greater supernatural force and accept the risks, bargains, and rewards described in the class text.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Hand to Hand must be bought as an other skill. Basic costs one selection, Expert costs two, and Martial Arts or Assassin costs three.

unresolvedNotes: Several starting shifter spells are listed here by name, but some are not yet present as imported spell items in the current catalog. Keep using the class list as written during play.
New shifter spells are learned or purchased manually after character creation rather than granted automatically by level.
If the player chooses a supernatural link, apply the extra P.P.E., S.D.C., and spell picks from the class text manually.

system.description: Arcane engineer who powers machines with P.P.E. and turns spellcraft into working gear, vehicles, and weapons.
system.notes: Play the character as an inventor first and a conventional spell caster second.

occSkills.name: Language
occSkills.notes: Choose two additional languages at +15% each.

relatedSkills.name: Basic Electronics
relatedSkills.notes: Each Electrical skill counts as two skill selections.

relatedSkills.name: Computer Repair
relatedSkills.notes: Each Electrical skill counts as two skill selections.

relatedSkills.name: Electrical Engineer
relatedSkills.notes: Each Electrical skill counts as two skill selections.

relatedSkills.name: Robot Electronics
relatedSkills.notes: Each Electrical skill counts as two skill selections.

relatedSkills.name: Aircraft Mechanics
relatedSkills.notes: Each Mechanical skill counts as two skill selections.

relatedSkills.name: Automotive Mechanics
relatedSkills.notes: Each Mechanical skill counts as two skill selections.

relatedSkills.name: Locksmith
relatedSkills.notes: Each Mechanical skill counts as two skill selections.

relatedSkills.name: Mechanical Engineer
relatedSkills.notes: Each Mechanical skill counts as two skill selections.

relatedSkills.name: Robot Mechanics
relatedSkills.notes: Each Mechanical skill counts as two skill selections.

relatedSkills.name: Weapons Engineer
relatedSkills.notes: Each Mechanical skill counts as two skill selections.

grantedAbilities.name: Spell Casting Penalty
grantedAbilities.notes: When casting a spell or ritual directly instead of channeling it through a Techno-Wizard device, halve range, duration, and damage as described in the class text.

grantedAbilities.name: Change Power Source to Magic P.P.E.
grantedAbilities.notes: Convert suitable devices to run on mystic or psionic energy using the conversion rules and costs from the class section.

grantedAbilities.name: Techno-Wizard Device Creation
grantedAbilities.notes: Build and modify Techno-Wizard devices using the tables and power links in the class rules.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Hand to Hand must be bought as an other skill. Basic costs one selection, Expert costs two, and Martial Arts or Assassin costs three.

unresolvedNotes: Electrical and Mechanical related skills each count as two selections, which is still a table-managed restriction.
The starting spell list includes Telekinesis by name, but that spell is not yet present as an imported spell item in the current catalog. Keep using the class list as written during play.
Roll starting money, black-market items, gems, and starting Techno-Wizard gear features from the class text.
